title: Bare keys cannot contain ':' during pull.
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Reported by @praveenkumar in https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/334.  Moving it here.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Today I am also trying out podman on fedora-28 and encounter the same issue. I do have atomic-registries not able to figure out what's wrong. I am happy to provide any other information which is need to debug it. Not sure should I reopen this or create a new issue.
```
$ sudo dnf --enablerepo updates-testing install podman

$ rpm -qa | grep atomic-registries
atomic-registries-1.22.1-2.fc28.x86_64

$ podman version
Version:       0.5.2
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

$ sudo podman pull fedora
error pulling image "fedora": unable to pull fedora: error getting default registries to try: Near line 9 (last key parsed ''): Bare keys cannot contain ':'.
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

See description

**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ sudo podman pull fedora
error pulling image "fedora": unable to pull fedora: error getting default registries to try: Near line 9 (last key parsed ''): Bare keys cannot contain ':'.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Pull would succeed

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:       0.5.2
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @praveenkumar can you supply the contents of /etc/container/registries.conf please?

We saw this issue a bit during the very early days of having a working `podman run` back in October - it was caused by a too-old `/etc/containers/registries.conf`. I think that's likely the issue here too.
Ooops, missed the 's' in containers in my reply.  But yeah, something is likely not right there as @mheon pointed out.

@TomSweeneyRedHat  @mheon  Here you go.

```
$ cat /etc/containers/registries.conf
# This is a system-wide configuration file used to
# keep track of registries for various container backends.
# It adheres to YAML format and does not support recursive
# lists of registries.

# The default location for this configuration file is /etc/containers/registries.conf.

# The only valid categories are: 'registries', 'insecure_registies', 
# and 'block_registries'.


registries:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com


# If you need to access insecure registries, uncomment the section below
# and add the registries fully-qualified name. An insecure registry is one
# that does not have a valid SSL certificate or only does HTTP.
insecure_registries:
  - 172.30.0.0/16

# If you need to block pull access from a registry, uncomment the section below
# and add the registries fully-qualified name.
#block_registries:
#  - 
```

And looks like below is what new format.

```
$ cat /etc/containers/registries.conf.rpmnew 
# This is a system-wide configuration file used to
# keep track of registries for various container backends.
# It adheres to TOML format and does not support recursive
# lists of registries.

# The default location for this configuration file is /etc/containers/registries.conf.

# The only valid categories are: 'registries.search', 'registries.insecure', 
# and 'registries.block'.

[registries.search]
registries = ['docker.io', 'registry.fedoraproject.org', 'quay.io', 'registry.access.redhat.com']

# If you need to access insecure registries, add the registry's fully-qualified name.
# An insecure registry is one that does not have a valid SSL certificate or only does HTTP.
[registries.insecure]
registries = []


# If you need to block pull access from a registry, uncomment the section below
# and add the registries fully-qualified name.
#
# Docker only
[registries.block]
registries = []

```
Shouldn't we document it somewhere as known issue? if there already existing known issue page then I can add it because for a normal user it hard to figure out what is causing it.

Updated the config file and now able to pull the images. Yay !!

```
$ sudo podman pull alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/alpine:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:ff3a5c916c92643ff77519ffa742d3ec61b7f591b6b7504599d95a4a41134e28
 1.97 MB / 1.97 MB [========================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:3fd9065eaf02feaf94d68376da52541925650b81698c53c6824d92ff63f98353
 1.48 KB / 1.48 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
3fd9065eaf02feaf94d68376da52541925650b81698c53c6824d92ff63f98353
[prkumar@local ~]$ sudo podman images
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/library/alpine   latest   3fd9065eaf02   4 months ago   4.41MB
```
@praveenkumar Yeah, we should probably stick a note in the tutorial about this.

@praveenkumar Thanks a bunch for the report, but more importantly the diagnosis.

@mheon I'll touch up the tutorials.  In the Buildah project we've a "Known issues"  page, I'll create one for podman and will add this as the first entry.
Should this be in man page?
So @TomSweeneyRedHat was @praveenkumar using the new format and it was failing?  The old format still works?

@rhatdan when @praveenkumar used the older format it failed.  With the new format like this: 
```
[registries.search]
registries = ['docker.io', 'registry.fedoraproject.org', 'quay.io', 'registry.access.redhat.com']
```
It works without an issue.  Man page add might be good, I'm just not quite sure where to put it and how/if we should document a known issue there on a man page or pages.
I though the sysregistries2 patches changed the format again? @mtrmac?

1. `sysregistriesv2` is, to my knowledge, not used anywhere yet.
2. `sysregistriesv2` does define a new format. To tell them apart, tests include headers like `[[registry]]`
3. `sysregistriesv2` should be able to transparently read the older `sysregistries` format (the one with `[registries.search]`.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Can you take care of this one?  Update the man pages?
Closing this as https://github.com/containers/image/pull/465 addressed the last little piece of this.
